verdict: 8 findings, 2 are P0 (fix this week). The leak is between interested and paid, not in traffic.

findings.what: No guest checkout — account creation is forced
findings.why: Forced account creation is one of the single biggest causes of checkout abandonment.
findings.fix: Enable guest checkout; offer account creation optionally after purchase.
findings.measure: Checkout completion rate before/after enabling guest checkout.

findings.what: Shipping cost is sprung at the final step
findings.why: Unexpected shipping at the last step is the #1 documented reason for cart abandonment.
findings.fix: Show shipping (or a threshold for free shipping) on the cart/PDP, before the final step.
findings.measure: Cart-to-purchase rate; abandonment at the shipping step should drop sharply.

findings.why: Every extra checkout step sheds buyers. Long checkouts convert worse, especially on mobile.
findings.fix: Collapse to a single-page or 2-3 step checkout; remove non-essential fields.
findings.measure: Completion rate per step; target fewer steps and higher overall completion.

findings.why: Shoppers abandon when their preferred method (wallet, UPI, BNPL, PayPal) is missing.
findings.fix: Add at least one wallet / express-pay option beside card.
findings.measure: Share of orders by method after adding options; watch completion lift.

findings.what: No reviews or ratings on the product page
findings.why: Social proof is decisive for D2C; an absent review block lowers conversion at the exact decision point.
findings.fix: Add ratings and reviews to the PDP; seed with real early-customer reviews.
findings.measure: Add-to-cart and purchase rate on PDPs with vs without reviews visible.

findings.what: Product copy is spec-led, not benefit-led
findings.why: Specs inform; benefits sell. Copy that only lists features underperforms.
findings.fix: Lead each section with the benefit, then support it with the spec.
findings.measure: A/B test benefit-led vs current copy on add-to-cart rate.

findings.what: No payment-security or trust badges at checkout
findings.why: Consumers look for security cues before entering card details.
findings.fix: Add recognised secure-payment badges near the payment field.
findings.measure: Checkout completion at the payment step.

findings.what: No honest urgency or scarcity cues
findings.why: Genuine low-stock or time-bound offers nudge decisions; none means fence-sitters drift.
findings.fix: Add honest stock counts or limited offers — never fake ones, which erode trust.
findings.measure: Add-to-cart rate on pages with vs without urgency cues.
